bull.b: Sustained, large free cash flow (~$9.8B and ~97% conversion) provides durable internal funding for buybacks, M&A, AI/AV investment and insurance/program changes. High cash conversion reduces refinancing risk and gives flexible capital allocation through cyclical headwinds.

Material multi-year scale demonstrates strong marketplace adoption and geographic breadth. Large gross bookings and revenues enable network effects, bargaining power with merchants and suppliers, and the operating leverage needed to absorb investments in AI, AV and new products over the medium term.

A fast-growing, large membership base that accounts for over half of bookings and drives 3x spend is a durable engagement moat. Recurring subscription behavior reduces elastic customer churn, improves lifetime value and lowers marginal acquisition costs across Mobility and Delivery.

bear.b: AV deployment promises long-term cost and unit-economics improvements but hinges on complex ecosystem buildout, regulatory approvals, capital for depots/charging and unresolved insurance/residual questions. Delays or higher-than-expected costs could push out expected productivity gains and returns.

While leverage improved from earlier years, the recent uptick and sizable absolute debt balance reduce financial flexibility. If growth or margins weaken, interest and principal servicing could constrain capital allocation for acquisitions, buybacks or elevated AI/AV investment plans.

Structural mix shift toward lower-margin segments (grocery, retail) and reaccelerating categories can cap overall margin expansion even as revenue grows. Persistent category mix effects and competitive pricing in Delivery may limit long-term operating-leverage gains unless offset by pricing or efficiency actions.
